Genista corymbosa, also known as Genista or Sweet-broom, is a shrub-like plant species in the Fabaceae family. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Genista corymbosa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are used to treat various health conditions.
